repo_prior_art.cite: none; the closest banked eid_proximal_completeness attempt did not supply this finite interaction-dimension formula.

published_prior_art.cite: YuShiTchetgen2025 defines H_gamma and gives one worked finite basis/matrix example, not a general dimension formula.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no catalogue entry proves a fortified-completeness cardinality obstruction.

published_prior_art.cite: YuShiTchetgen2025 assumes fortified completeness for identification; ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 concerns nonunique bridge solutions, not this finite supply-demand barrier.
A monotonicity corollary of a corrected closed dimension formula; no cited paper states this frontier.

repo_prior_art.cite: none; prior proximal-completeness notes identified realization as unresolved but did not formulate this positive-minor route for the fortified pairing.

published_prior_art.cite: AllmanMatiasRhodes2009 and Gu2025BlessingDependence provide relevant generic-identifiability methods in different latent models, not this fortified-proxy pairing result.

soundness_flags.label: Assumption ass:strict-cell-positivity
soundness_flags.one_line: YuShiTchetgen2025's named positivity condition is treatment positivity conditional on U and the complement proxy vector, not the proposal's full-cell condition P(U,A,Z)>0; correct the citation/name or retag and justify the stronger finite-support regularity.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 (interaction dimension)
soundness_flags.one_line: The asserted 'direct interaction decomposition' is undefined for an arbitrary dependent positive law; specify a P-weighted/effect-coded tensor decomposition rather than invoking unqualified interactions.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / anchor special case K=2,m=3,u=2,gamma=1
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed formula gives D=3^2+(3-1)^2+3=16, whereas YuShiTchetgen2025's worked Assumption-5 illustration calls the corresponding matrix 12-by-15; reconcile the differing space/basis or establish that the anchor contains a typo before claiming an exact formula.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 (generic attainability)
soundness_flags.one_line: The question quantifies over alpha in A_gamma but only assumes |alpha^star|>=gamma, never binds alpha=alpha^star; thus it need not test the valid-proxy component whose complement C_alpha defines the demand space.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 2 / technical_internal_limitation
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Negative slack is accepted only as failure of the fortified outcome-completeness pairing, not as ATE nonidentification.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: For every strictly positive law in the stated finite model, the anchor's Assumption-5 outcome-bridge injectivity cannot hold when the corrected supply dimension is below the demand dimension; no query-level nonidentification follows.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Nonnegative slack is not assumed sufficient; compatibility with proxy restrictions and generic full rank remain open.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The settled contribution is a necessary finite-support barrier and monotone necessary frontier, conditional on the corrected dimension theorem.

source_verification_receipts.locator: pp. 5-6, Definition 2.1(ii); pp. 9-11, equation (2.3), Assumption 5, Remark 2.3
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: Definition 2.1(ii) requires 0<P(A=a|U,Z_{-nu*},X)<1, while Assumption 5 pairs H_gamma+L2(Z_{-nu*},X) with L2(U,A,Z_{-nu*},X); the K=2 ternary/binary-U illustration describes a 12-by-15 full-rank matrix.
The paper states that at least two independent proxy variables satisfying a rank condition can nonparametrically identify the causal effect.

source_verification_receipts.version: Author manuscript of Statistics and Probability Letters 198 (2023), 109836
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper studies existence and nonuniqueness of proximal bridge solutions, constructs a solution-set estimator, and gives root-n efficient estimation under additional conditions.

source_verification_receipts.version: arXiv preprint corresponding to Annals of Statistics 37 (2009), 3099-3132
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper develops algebraic generic-identifiability arguments for structured latent-variable models, centered on Kruskal-type methods.
The paper proves generic-identifiability results and identifies exceptional dependence structures in the BLESS discrete latent model; it is methodological, not a fortified-proxy rank theorem.

source_verification_receipts.version: Journal of the American Statistical Association 119(547) (2024), 1778-1793
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The paper gives a general automated polynomial-programming approach for sharp causal bounds in discrete settings, distinct from the proposal's route-level completeness feasibility question.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Derive the K=2,m=3,u=2,gamma=1 basis explicitly and resolve the anchor's 12-by-15 count; then state the general dimension theorem using a defined P-weighted tensor basis.
Bind each attainability component to alpha=alpha-star (preferably treat the worst-case exact-cardinality alpha-star in A_gamma), and define the finite latent-completion model including W,Y(0),Y(1).
Replace the full-cell positivity citation with an independently justified finite-support regularity condition, or formulate a support-adaptive version whose dimensions use effective support sizes.

Strengthen the eventual positive half into an iff feasibility theorem with an explicit nonzero minor or finite algorithm for constructing a compatible positive law, rather than generic existence alone.

recommended_next_step: Revise the exact dimension kernel first: reconcile the cited 12-by-15 example, define the weighted interaction construction, and bind the generic-attainability law to the actual valid-proxy set before advancing to Stage 0.
